reasoning: Fidelity composite critically low: 0.40 (3/3 dimensions evaluated). Document quality below minimum threshold for publication.

concerns.analysis: The output is missing a significant portion of the document. Specifically, Sections 1.1, 1.2, 1.3, 2.1, 2.2, and the entirety of Section 3 are absent, replaced by a token budget omission placeholder. Additionally, Page 8 containing Table 2 (Study Group members) is missing entirely from the content stream.

Three significant table failures were identified. 1) Table 3 (Population Breakdown) on page 9 is missing entirely. 2) The Appendix B table, which should contain 100 counties, only contains the first 14 rows (over 80% missing). 3) The Appendix C table is severely truncated, showing only 4 entries out of dozens present in the source document (over 90% missing).
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Table 3 – Breakdown of North Carolina Population, 2020 (Page 9); Appendix B full list of 100 counties (Pages 46-48); Appendix C full list of standards (Pages 49-50).
concerns.output_quote: Appendix B table ends at Caldwell County (reading_order 23); Appendix C table contains only 4 rows (reading_order 23).

concerns.analysis: The meaningful technical diagram 'Figure 1 – Simplified NENA i3 NG911 Conceptual System Diagram' found on page 24 of the source PDF is entirely missing from the HTML output. While the caption text is present, the graphical information representing the system architecture is gone. Additionally, the photo of the North Carolina flag on the cover page is missing, though this is less critical than the technical diagram.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 24 contains 'Figure 1 – Simplified NENA i3 NG911 Conceptual System Diagram' which is a complex technical flowchart.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML output shows the text caption for Figure 1, but no image or SVG diagram is rendered in that section.

concerns.description: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: I found one instance of date truncation in the footnotes. Footnote 11 lists the date as 'August 5, 201' instead of 'August 5, 2013'. This is a character-level truncation that alters the information provided.
